title: Anger at the Door — The Story of Cain
reading:
God spoke to Cain before it happened. Genesis 4:6-7 — Why are you angry? And why has your countenance fallen? If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin lies at the door. And its desire is for you, but you should rule over it.

Cain had a choice to make. Would he allow anger to control him, or would he humble himself before God and listen to His voice?

He didn't listen. Genesis 4:8 — Cain rose up against Abel his brother and killed him.

Because anger was left unchecked, rage took over. It blinded Cain, causing him to kill his own brother out of jealousy. You can only imagine the thoughts consuming him — God only cares about Abel and not me. I deserved to be favored. Why couldn't God accept my offering as well?

Anger blinds us to our own actions. The result is absolutely destructive. It brought Cain to murdering the very brother he was supposed to love and care for.

The most sobering part of this story is that God warned him. Sin was crouching at the door, and God told him to rule over it. He had every opportunity to choose differently.

Anger will always give you a moment — a door — where you can still choose. The question is whether you take it.
reflection: God told Cain that anger was at the door before he acted. Can you identify the moments in your own life when anger is crouching — before it takes over? What does it feel like, and what do you do with that moment?
abide: Lord, help me recognize the door before I walk through it. Give me the grace to choose You over my anger, even when everything in me wants to give in.
